last_assistant_message:
Task 2.1 complete.

## Report

**Status:** PASS — all gates green.

**Files created (3):**
- `C:\work\mega\memory\.claude\worktrees\feat-transcript-ingest\src\AstraMemory.Domain\Interfaces\ITranscriptExtractor.cs`
- `C:\work\mega\memory\.claude\worktrees\feat-transcript-ingest\src\AstraMemory.Infrastructure\Services\TranscriptExtractor.cs`
- `C:\work\mega\memory\.claude\worktrees\feat-transcript-ingest\tests\AstraMemory.Tests\TranscriptIngest\TranscriptExtractorTests.cs`

**Build/test output:**
- Initial test run: FAIL (5 CS0246 errors — `TranscriptExtractor` undefined) as expected.
- After implementation: 5/5 PASS (TranscriptExtractorTests, 82 ms).
- Regression suite: 18/18 PASS (TranscriptScrubberTests + IdempotencyCacheTests + TranscriptIngestServiceTests + TranscriptIngestControllerTests, 89 ms).

**Commit:** `ad1553a3` — `feat(api): TranscriptExtractor emits decision/fact/lesson/event atoms`. Parent was `e891bfd3`. Exactly 3 files, +195 lines.

**Self-review:**
- `ITranscriptExtractor` lives in `AstraMemory.Domain.Interfaces` — only depends on BCL types (no Infrastructure leakage).
- `TranscriptExtractor` is `sealed`, lives in `AstraMemory.Infrastructure.Services`, depends only on `Astra.Core.LLM.ILlmCompletionProvider` and the Domain interface — same pattern as sibling `EngineeringExtractionService`.
- `ExtractedAtom` record carries all six fields: Type, Title, Content, Importance, EntityRefs, SupersedesHint.
- `Parse` rejects unknown types (only `decision|fact|lesson|event`), rejects blank titles, tolerates missing fields (defaults importance to 0.5, refs to empty, supersedes to null), and returns `[]` on `JsonException`.
- Blank-input fast path verified via `Times.Never` on `CompleteAsync`.

**LLM provider signature:** No concerns. `ILlmCompletionProvider.CompleteAsync(string systemPrompt, string userPrompt, CancellationToken ct)` matches exactly the signature used in `EngineeringExtractionService.cs` line 38 and matches the task spec. `IsAvailable` getter is present and used identically. No adaptation was needed.
